description: Add a CLI tool interface and an MCP gateway endpoint so AI agents can choose how to interact with OpenTabs: (1) full MCP with all tools registered, (2) gateway MCP with just 2 meta-tools (opentabs_list_tools + opentabs_call), or (3) CLI-only via shell commands (opentabs tool list/call). All three interfaces share the same running server and dispatch pipeline.

userStories.title: Add GET /tools HTTP endpoint for tool discovery
userStories.description: Add a new authenticated HTTP endpoint GET /tools that returns all available plugin and browser tools with their full JSON schemas. Supports optional query params for filtering by plugin and selecting specific fields.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /tools returns a JSON array of all tools with name, description, pluginName, and inputSchema
GET /tools?plugin=slack filters to tools from the slack plugin only
GET /tools?plugin=browser filters to browser tools only
GET /tools requires bearer auth (same as /health)
Returns 401 without valid auth, 200 with valid auth

userStories.title: Add POST /tools/:name/call HTTP endpoint for tool invocation
userStories.description: Add a new authenticated HTTP endpoint POST /tools/:name/call that invokes any tool by its prefixed name, dispatches it through the existing pipeline, and returns the result as JSON.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /tools/slack_send_message/call with JSON body {"arguments":{...}} invokes the tool and returns the result
POST /tools/browser_list_tabs/call works for browser tools
Returns the tool result content as JSON (same shape as MCP tools/call response)
Returns 401 without auth, 404 for unknown tools, 400 for invalid body
Respects the same permission model as MCP dispatch (off → error, ask → error with message, auto → execute)
Supports tabId and instance parameters in the arguments object

userStories.title: Add opentabs tool list CLI command
userStories.description: Add a new 'opentabs tool list' CLI command that fetches tools from the running server via GET /tools and displays them. Supports --json for machine-readable output, --plugin for filtering, and human-readable table output by default.
acceptanceCriteria: opentabs tool list displays all tools in a human-readable format with name, plugin, and description
opentabs tool list --json outputs the full tool list with schemas as JSON
opentabs tool list --plugin slack filters to tools from the slack plugin
When the server is not running, shows an error with 'opentabs start' hint
Output is piped cleanly (no colors when stdout is not a TTY)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/cli/src/commands/tool.ts (new file)
- platform/cli/src/commands/index.ts (add export for registerToolCommand)
- platform/cli/src/cli.ts (register the tool command)

Context:
The CLI entry point (cli.ts) registers commands via functions like `registerPluginCommand(program)`. Follow the same pattern.

The CLI already has a helper pattern for talking to the server. Look at how `handlePluginList` in plugin.ts (line 561) fetches from `/health`:
```ts
const headers: Record<string, string> = {};
if (secret) headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${secret}`;
const res = await fetch(`http://${DEFAULT_HOST}:${port}/health`, {
  headers,
  signal: AbortSignal.timeout(3_000),
});
```

Use the same pattern to fetch from `/tools`.

For the command structure:
```ts
const registerToolCommand = (program: Command): void => {
  const toolCmd = program
    .command('tool')
    .description('Discover and invoke plugin tools')
    .action(() => toolCmd.help());

  toolCmd
    .command('list')
    .alias('ls')
    .description('List available tools from the running server')
    .option('--port <number>', 'Server port', parsePort)
    .option('--json', 'Output full tool schemas as JSON')
    .option('--plugin <name>', 'Filter by plugin name')
    .action(...);
};
```

Human-readable output format (match the style of `opentabs plugin list`):
```
Available Tools

  slack (Slack) — 5 tools
    slack_send_message          Send a message to a Slack channel
    slack_read_messages         Read recent messages from a channel
    slack_search_messages       Search messages across channels
    slack_list_channels         List channels in the workspace
    slack_get_user_info         Get user profile information

  browser (Browser) — 12 tools
    browser_list_tabs           List all open browser tabs
    browser_screenshot_tab      Take a screenshot of a tab
    ...
```

JSON output: the raw array from GET /tools.

Non-TTY detection: use `process.stdout.isTTY` to strip colors (picocolors handles this automatically).

Constraints:
- Follow the existing CLI patterns (readAuthSecret, resolvePort, DEFAULT_HOST, isConnectionRefused)
- Group tools by plugin in human-readable mode
- Show tool count per plugin
- Use picocolors for styling, matching the plugin list style

userStories.title: Add opentabs tool schema CLI command
userStories.description: Add 'opentabs tool schema <name>' CLI command that fetches and displays the full JSON schema for a specific tool.
acceptanceCriteria: opentabs tool schema slack_send_message outputs the tool's full JSON input schema
Output is always JSON (pretty-printed)
Unknown tool name shows an error with available tools hint
Includes the tool description alongside the schema

userStories.title: Add opentabs tool call CLI command
userStories.description: Add 'opentabs tool call <name> [json]' CLI command that invokes a tool on the running server via POST /tools/:name/call and prints the result.
acceptanceCriteria: opentabs tool call slack_send_message '{"channel":"C123","text":"hi"}' invokes the tool and prints the result as JSON
Arguments can be passed as a positional JSON string or via --params flag
Exit code is 0 on success, 1 on tool error (isError: true), 2 on HTTP/connection errors
Result content is printed to stdout as JSON
Error messages are printed to stderr
Supports --instance and --tab-id flags that get merged into the arguments
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/cli/src/commands/tool.ts (add subcommand to the tool command group)

Context:
This is the core command for CLI-mode tool invocation.

```ts
toolCmd
  .command('call')
  .description('Invoke a tool on the running server')
  .argument('<name>', 'Tool name (e.g., slack_send_message, browser_list_tabs)')
  .argument('[json]', 'Tool arguments as a JSON string')
  .option('--params <json>', 'Tool arguments as JSON (alternative to positional arg)')
  .option('--instance <name>', 'Target a named instance (for multi-instance plugins)')
  .option('--tab-id <id>', 'Target a specific browser tab by ID', parseInt)
  .option('--port <number>', 'Server port', parsePort)
  .action(async (name: string, jsonArg: string | undefined, _options: unknown, command: Command) => {
    const opts = command.optsWithGlobals();
    // Parse arguments from positional or --params
    // Merge --instance and --tab-id into args
    // POST to /tools/<name>/call
    // Print result to stdout
    // Set exit code based on isError
  });
```

Argument parsing:
- Positional JSON: `opentabs tool call slack_send_message '{"channel":"C123"}'`
- Flag JSON: `opentabs tool call slack_send_message --params '{"channel":"C123"}'`
- Flag takes precedence if both are provided
- If neither, args = {} (empty object)

Instance and tabId merging:
```ts
const args = { ...parsedJson };
if (opts.instance) args.instance = opts.instance;
if (opts.tabId) args.tabId = opts.tabId;
```

Output:
- Success: print the content array's text fields, joined by newlines, to stdout
- If the content is valid JSON, pretty-print it
- Error: print to stderr, exit with code 1
- Connection error: print 'Server not running' hint to stderr, exit with code 2

Constraints:
- Invalid JSON in the positional arg should give a clear error: 'Invalid JSON: ...' to stderr
- Do NOT prompt for confirmation on `ask` tools — the server's ask flow works via the side panel. The HTTP endpoint handles this (US-002).
- The output should be clean JSON to stdout with no decorative text, so it's pipeable: `opentabs tool call ... | jq '.field'`

userStories.title: Add MCP gateway endpoint at /mcp/gateway
userStories.description: Add a second MCP endpoint at /mcp/gateway that registers only 2 meta-tools: opentabs_list_tools and opentabs_call. This gives AI agents full OpenTabs capability through minimal context footprint.
acceptanceCriteria: Connecting to /mcp/gateway via MCP protocol shows exactly 2 tools: opentabs_list_tools and opentabs_call
opentabs_list_tools returns available tools with schemas, supports optional plugin filter
opentabs_call invokes any tool by name, returns the tool result
Both meta-tools use the same dispatch pipeline as full MCP (permissions, validation, tab targeting)
/mcp continues to work as before with all tools
Both endpoints require the same bearer auth

userStories.title: Update opentabs start to show all three connection options
userStories.description: Update the start command's MCP client configuration output to show full MCP, gateway MCP, and CLI-only options.
acceptanceCriteria: opentabs start prints three clearly labeled sections: Full MCP, Gateway MCP, and CLI-only
Each section shows the correct URL/command for the respective mode
Gateway section shows /mcp/gateway URL for each client
CLI section shows example opentabs tool commands
opentabs config show also displays all three options
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts (getMcpClientConfigs, printMcpClientConfigs, printFirstTimeInstructions)

Context:
The current `getMcpClientConfigs` (line 180) returns configs for Claude Code, OpenCode, Cursor, and Windsurf with the `/mcp` URL. `printMcpClientConfigs` (line 223) iterates and prints them.

Changes:
Update `printMcpClientConfigs` to print three sections:

```
  Connection modes:

  Full MCP (all tools — recommended for most users):
     claude mcp add --transport http opentabs http://127.0.0.1:9515/mcp \
       --header "Authorization: Bearer abc123"

  Gateway MCP (2 meta-tools — minimal context footprint):
     claude mcp add --transport http opentabs-gateway http://127.0.0.1:9515/mcp/gateway \
       --header "Authorization: Bearer abc123"

  CLI only (no MCP registration — use shell commands):
     opentabs tool list                              # discover tools
     opentabs tool call <name> '{"key": "value"}'    # invoke a tool
```

For the non-Claude clients, show the gateway URL option too.

Also update the first-time instructions (printFirstTimeInstructions, line 158) to include the gateway and CLI options.

Constraints:
- Keep the existing full MCP config as the primary/recommended option
- Use picocolors for styling consistently with the current output
- Do NOT change the server startup logic — just the printed instructions

userStories.title: Add unit tests for HTTP tool endpoints
userStories.description: Add comprehensive unit tests for the GET /tools and POST /tools/:name/call HTTP endpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests for GET /tools: auth required, returns all tools, plugin filter works, annotates tools with plugin name
Tests for POST /tools/:name/call: auth required, rate limited, dispatches to correct handler, returns result, handles unknown tools, handles empty body
Tests follow existing patterns in http-routes.test.ts
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.test.ts (add test cases)

Context:
Existing test patterns in http-routes.test.ts:
- Create state: `const state = createState();`
- Set auth: `state.wsSecret = 'test-secret';`
- Build registry with plugins: `state.registry = buildRegistry([createPlugin(...)]);`
- Create handlers: `const { handleRequest } = createHandlers({ state, transports, sessionServers, getHotState });`
- Make requests: `const req = new Request('http://localhost/tools', { headers: { Authorization: 'Bearer test-secret' } });`
- Assert: `const res = await handleRequest(req, ...); expect(res.status).toBe(200);`

The `createPlugin` helper (from mcp-setup.test.ts) creates a minimal RegisteredPlugin. Import or duplicate it.

Test cases for GET /tools:
1. Without auth → 401
2. With auth, no plugins → returns browser tools + platform tools only
3. With auth, with plugins → returns all tools annotated with plugin names
4. With ?plugin=slack filter → returns only slack tools
5. With ?plugin=browser filter → returns only browser tools
6. With ?plugin=nonexistent → returns empty array

Test cases for POST /tools/:name/call:
1. Without auth → 401
2. Unknown tool → 404 with error message
3. Invalid URL format (no /call suffix) → 404
4. Valid browser tool call → dispatches and returns (mock the handler)
5. Valid plugin tool call → dispatches and returns (mock the handler)
6. Empty body → uses empty args
7. Rate limiting after 30 calls → 429

Note: For POST /tools/:name/call tests, the actual dispatch requires an extension connection. For unit tests, mock `dispatchToExtension` or test only the routing/validation logic. The E2E tests (US-010) will cover the full dispatch.

Constraints:
- Follow existing test patterns exactly
- Do NOT use external test fixtures — create state inline

userStories.title: Add unit tests for MCP gateway meta-tools
userStories.description: Add unit tests for the gateway MCP server's opentabs_list_tools and opentabs_call meta-tools.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests verify opentabs_list_tools returns all tools with schemas
Tests verify opentabs_list_tools plugin filter works
Tests verify opentabs_call routes to the correct dispatch handler
Tests verify opentabs_call returns tool errors properly
Tests verify the gateway tools/list response has exactly 2 tools
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-gateway.test.ts (new file)

Context:
Follow the pattern from mcp-setup.test.ts for creating mock state with plugins and browser tools.

The gateway module exports functions that can be tested directly:
- The list_tools handler function
- The call handler function
- The GATEWAY_TOOLS constant (verify shape)

Test cases for opentabs_list_tools:
1. No plugin filter → returns all tools (plugins + browser + platform)
2. Plugin filter 'slack' → returns only slack tools
3. Plugin filter 'browser' → returns only browser tools
4. Each tool in result has name, description, plugin, inputSchema

Test cases for opentabs_call:
1. Call with tool: 'browser_list_tabs' → routes to browser tool handler
2. Call with tool: 'slack_send_message' → routes to plugin tool handler
3. Call with unknown tool → returns error content
4. Call without tool field → returns error
5. Call with arguments → passes arguments through to handler

Test cases for gateway server:
1. Gateway tools/list → exactly 2 tools
2. Tool names are 'opentabs_list_tools' and 'opentabs_call'

Constraints:
- Mock the dispatch functions to avoid needing an extension connection
- Test the routing logic, not the actual dispatch (E2E tests cover that)

E2E test verifies POST /tools/e2e-test_get_status/call dispatches to the test plugin and returns a result
E2E test verifies the gateway endpoint serves exactly 2 tools
E2E test verifies opentabs_call through the gateway dispatches correctly
E2E test verifies opentabs_list_tools through the gateway returns plugin tools
